demographics.living_situation: lives with their partner, who is present and helped make contact

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My whole body feels like it's been beaten up — my neck, shoulders and lower back are in agony, my head feels like it's being crushed, and I'm so wiped out I can't get out of bed. I've been to doctors before about this and keep getting told there's nothing physically wrong, but tonight I can barely move.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The pain and the headache got unbearable tonight and I'm stuck in bed — I need help and I don't know what else to do.

symptom_narrative.onset: the aching and headaches built up over the past week; tonight it became unbearable
symptom_narrative.duration: about a week, much worse in the last few hours
symptom_narrative.character: deep, bruised, all-over aching in the neck, shoulders and lower back, with a crushing, pressing headache
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it has been — I can't get out of bed and even small movements make me grimace
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: neck, shoulders, lower back, and a band of pressure around and at the back of my head
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there most of the time and flares up; it feels worse when everything in the news and the world feels heavier
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: almost nothing — lying still in a dark room helps a little
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: totally drained even after sleeping, an upset stomach, and some pins-and-needles tingling in my arms
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had waves of this kind of pain over several years and been checked before, with nothing found
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: bedbound tonight; can't do basic tasks or care for myself

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: long-running episodes of widespread body pain that have been investigated before with no cause found

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: taking it on and off for the last few days
taking it pretty much continuously for about 3 days with barely any relief

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: belongs to a community that faces ongoing discrimination and has felt worn down by recent distressing news; describes years of feeling dismissed and not believed by health services, which the patient finds re-traumatising. The partner is supportive and says the symptoms seem to flare when things in the wider world feel heavier.

communication_style.typical_message_style: articulate and frustrated; frames the problem partly in terms of being repeatedly let down by the system

reluctance_topics: mood and thoughts of self-harm unless asked gently and directly

objective_data_offered.value: 36.8 degrees on our home thermometer, so no fever that we can tell
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: single reading on an unvalidated home thermometer; a normal home temperature does NOT reliably exclude infection or meningitis

objective_data_offered.value: moving very stiffly and grimacing with small changes in position, repeatedly rubbing the back of the neck
dark towels have been hung over the windows because light is hurting the eyes with the headache
partner reports the physical symptoms seem to rise and fall with how stressful things in the news and the wider world are
